Name: Cape Fresh Produce Market – Cold Chain – Strawberries Stellenbosch – 8,400 kg, 0–2°C
Description: Cold chain event for 8,400 kg fresh strawberries (class 1, Elsanta/Albion varieties) from Stellenbosch berry farms received at CFPM pre-cool tunnel, temperature reduced from 18°C to 0–2°C within 3 hours. IoT logger CFPM-TMP-2025-0012 hash verified. PPECB inspection sticker No. PHY-2025-0034.

Name: Cape Fresh Produce Market – Cold Chain – Hass Avocado Tzaneen – 24,000 kg, 5–7°C
Description: Cold chain integrity event for 24,000 kg stage-1 Hass avocados from Tzaneen (Limpopo) in 6 reefer trucks, received and inspected at CFPM, temperature pre-cooled to 5–7°C. PPECB Grade 1 certificate PHY-2025-0112. GlobalG.A.P. certificate GP-ZA-2025-0078 attached.

Name: Cape Fresh Produce Market – Cold Chain – Blueberries Ceres – 3,200 kg, 0–2°C
Description: Cold chain event for 3,200 kg Southern Highbush blueberries (class 1) from Ceres Valley farms, pre-cooled at CFPM cold store to 0–2°C for domestic supermarket distribution. IoT logger hash CFPM-TMP-2025-0056. ZQA Berry quality certificate. 14-day shelf-life from pack date.

Name: Cape Fresh Produce Market – Cold Chain – Table Grapes Hex River – 18,600 kg, 0–1°C
Description: Cold chain event: 18,600 kg table grapes (Crimson Seedless, Thompson Seedless) from Hex River Valley pack-house received at CFPM under modified atmosphere (MA) at 0–1°C. SO₂ pad treatment log attached. PPECB Grade 1 inspection PHY-2025-0178. Export-grade qualification for EU market verified.

Name: Cape Fresh Produce Market – Cold Chain – Export Pears Elgin – 14,000 kg, 0°C Controlled Atmosphere
Description: Cold chain event for 14,000 kg packham and forelle pears from Elgin Valley (CA store exit, grade 1) received at CFPM CA room for onward export to UK and Middle East. Temperature 0°C, CO₂ 2.5%, O₂ 1.5%. Packing audit certificate. PPECB PHY-2025-0234 and GlobalG.A.P. cert.

Name: Cape Fresh Produce Market – Cold Chain – Nectarines & Peaches Ceres – 9,800 kg, 0–1°C
Description: Cold chain event for 9,800 kg nectarines and white clingstone peaches (grade 1) from Ceres stone-fruit farms pre-cooled at CFPM to 0–1°C for domestic supermarket chain Checkers. PPECB certificate PHY-2025-0289. Brix and firmness quality report signed by packer and CFPM inspector.

Name: Cape Fresh Produce Market – Cold Chain – Organic Spinach & Kale Piketberg – 4,200 kg, 2–4°C
Description: Cold chain event for 4,200 kg certified organic spinach and kale (ECOCERT cert ORC-2025-0045) from Piketberg small-scale organic farms, pre-cooled to 2–4°C at CFPM. IoT logger CFPM-TMP-2025-0167. 5-day shelf-life post-harvest. Organic certification number: ORC-ZA-2025-0045.

Name: Cape Fresh Produce Market – Cold Chain – Export Apples Grabouw – 28,000 kg, 0°C CA
Description: Cold chain event for 28,000 kg Granny Smith and Fuji apples (grade 1, CA store, Grabouw, Elgin) received at CFPM CA terminal for export to Europe and Asia. Temperature 0°C, CO₂ 1.5%, O₂ 1.8%. PPECB certificate PHY-2025-0345. Fruit SA export audit. GlobalG.A.P. record attached.

Name: Cape Fresh Produce Market – Cold Chain – Soft Citrus Satsuma Citrusdal – 12,400 kg, 6–8°C
Description: Cold chain event for 12,400 kg Satsuma mandarin and Clementine (seedless, easy-peel) from Citrusdal, pre-cooled to 6–8°C at CFPM. 21-day shelf-life. PPECB phytosanitary PHY-2025-0412 and DFAM (degreening certificate) DA-2025-0178. Destination: Woolworths Food SA.

Name: Cape Fresh Produce Market – Cold Chain – Export Cherries Robertson – 2,100 kg, 0–1°C
Description: Cold chain event for 2,100 kg Bing and Brooks cherries (class 1, ≥28mm) from Robertson Valley farms pre-cooled at CFPM to 0–1°C under modified atmosphere. PPECB phytosanitary PHY-2025-0456. Cherries SA export audit. Air-cargo manifest CFPM-CHR-2025-0012. Destination: Taiwan, Hong Kong, Germany.

Name: CFPM Phytosanitary Inspection – Citrus Bay 12A – Navel Oranges – 38,000 kg
Description: CFPM PPECB-accredited inspector conducted phytosanitary inspection on 38,000 kg Navel oranges (Valencias from Citrusdal, class 1) in Bay 12A. Zero citrus black spot, no thrips damage. PPECB inspection report CFPM-PHY-2025-0067. Passed for EU export under Regulation (EU) 2022/2379.

Name: CFPM Phytosanitary Inspection – Deciduous Bay 7B – Granny Smith Apples – 56,000 kg
Description: PPECB-accredited inspection for 56,000 kg Granny Smith apples (class 1, Grabouw) in Bay 7B. Zero core flush, zero bitter pit above threshold. CFPM-PHY-2025-0089. Fruit maturity report: starch index 3.5, firmness 8.2 kg/cm². Passed for Asia (China) export protocol.

Name: CFPM Food Safety Inspection – Bay 3C – Tomatoes Wolseley – 22,000 kg
Description: CFPM food safety inspection for 22,000 kg beef tomatoes (class 1) from Wolseley. HACCP trace-back record. Pesticide residue MRL test results (zero detectable above EU MRL). CFPM inspector Mr. Wessel Hanekom. DALRRD food safety cert DA-FS-2025-0112 issued. Destined for Woolworths SA distribution.

Name: CFPM Phytosanitary Inspection – Bay 9A – Mangoes Limpopo – 14,600 kg
Description: PPECB phytosanitary inspection for 14,600 kg Tommy Atkins mangoes (class 1, Tzaneen) in Bay 9A. Vapour heat treatment (VHT) records from packing facility verified (47°C core temp). CFPM-PHY-2025-0145. Passed for export to Japan under Japan phytosanitary protocol. Residue test MRL compliant.

Name: CFPM Phytosanitary Inspection – Bay 15B – Table Grapes Robertson – 32,000 kg
Description: CFPM PPECB inspection for 32,000 kg Crimson Seedless grapes (class 1) from Robertson for export to UK (Tesco supply protocol). Zero scale insects, zero bunch stem decay. CFPM-PHY-2025-0189. GlobalG.A.P. audit record GP-ZA-2025-0123. Modified atmosphere (MA) bag integrity verified.

Name: CFPM Food Safety Inspection – Bay 6D – Broccoli & Cauliflower Villiersdorp – 8,400 kg
Description: CFPM food safety inspection for 8,400 kg broccoli and cauliflower (class 1, Villiersdorp) for Checkers domestic supply. Pesticide residue nil above MRL. Temperature compliance 4°C verified. HACCP record CFPM-FS-2025-0234 and DALRRD certificate DA-FS-2025-0145 issued.

Name: CFPM Phytosanitary Inspection – Bay 11A – Stonefruit Paarl – 16,200 kg
Description: CFPM PPECB inspection for 16,200 kg stonefruit (nectarines, peaches, plums) from Paarl farms for export to Middle East. Zero brown rot, zero scale insects. Temperature compliance 0–1°C. CFPM-PHY-2025-0267 and GlobalG.A.P. cert GP-ZA-2025-0167. Residue test certified MRL compliant.

Name: CFPM Organic Certification Inspection – Bay 2A – Organic Citrus Swellendam – 9,600 kg
Description: CFPM ECOCERT-accredited organic inspection for 9,600 kg certified organic lemons and Navels from Swellendam (cert ORC-ZA-2025-0089). Zero synthetic pesticide residues confirmed. PPECB organic export label EP-ORG-2025-0089. Intended for Waitrose UK organic shelf.

Name: CFPM – Delivery – Avocado Limpopo to Cape Town Market – 14,200 kg
Description: Road delivery of 14,200 kg stage-1 Hass avocados from Tzaneen packing shed to Cape Fresh Produce Market in 2 reefer trucks (7,100 kg each). Temperature maintained 5–7°C throughout 1,800 km journey. Delivery manifest CFPM-DLV-2025-0045. PPECB grade certificate attached.

Name: CFPM – Delivery – Granny Smith Apples Grabouw to CFPM for Export Packing – 28,000 kg
Description: Delivery of 28,000 kg Granny Smith apples (grade 1) from Grabouw cold store to CFPM export packing hall for sorting and export to Asia. Refrigerated truck manifest CFPM-DLV-2025-0078. Temperature log verified 0°C. WeightBridge certificate attached.

Name: CFPM – Delivery – Mango Limpopo to CFPM Wholesale – 8,600 kg
Description: 8,600 kg Tommy Atkins mangoes (class 1, Tzaneen) delivered by refrigerated truck from Limpopo pack-house to CFPM wholesale bay. Temperature 10–13°C. VHT treatment record attached. CFPM-DLV-2025-0112. Broker delivery note: Evergreen Produce Sales (Pty) Ltd.

Name: CFPM – Delivery – Citrus Stellenbosch to CFPM Retail Bays – 18,000 kg
Description: 18,000 kg soft citrus (Satsuma mandarin, Clementines) from Stellenbosch co-op delivery to CFPM retail bays for supermarket distribution. 3 reefer trucks. Temperature 6–8°C. CFPM-DLV-2025-0145. Retailer allocation: Pick n Pay 60%, Spar 40%.

Name: CFPM – Delivery – Organic Spinach & Kale Piketberg to CFPM – 3,600 kg
Description: 3,600 kg certified organic spinach and kale from Piketberg small-scale farms delivered to CFPM organic bay in refrigerated van. Temperature 2–4°C. ECOCERT cert number ORC-ZA-2025-0045. CFPM-DLV-2025-0167. Client: Woolworths Food SA organic range.

Name: CFPM – Delivery – Table Grapes Robertson to CFPM for EU Export Packing – 32,000 kg
Description: 32,000 kg Crimson Seedless table grapes (class 1, MA packs) from Robertson pack-house to CFPM EU export packing line for Tesco protocol. 4 reefer trucks, 8,000 kg each. Temperature 0–1°C. CFPM-DLV-2025-0189. GlobalG.A.P. record GP-ZA-2025-0123.

Name: CFPM – Delivery – Cherries Robertson to CFPM for Asia Export – 2,100 kg
Description: 2,100 kg Bing and Brooks cherries (class 1, ≥28mm) from Robertson airfreight pack-house to CFPM for final sorting and air-cargo handover to Cape Town International Airport. Temperature 0–1°C. CFPM-DLV-2025-0223. Air-cargo manifest booked with Ethiopian Airlines cargo EAC-2025-0112.
